title: DNase I Treatment
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9lnrp","text":"This protocol describes the preparation of and treatment with DNase I to degrade DNA in solutions containing iron chloride, EDTA–Mg ascorbate buffer, and cesium chloride. DNase I treatment, CsCl purification, and sucrose purification methods were compared using replicated viral metagenomics in Hurwitz et al. 2012.  We use this protocol primarily to remove free host and other contaminating DNA from phage preparations prior to extracting nucleic acid from the phage particles.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"c23nj","text":"Resuspending DNase I from powder","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":32}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b1uib","text":"Determine number of units per mg powder: Roche grade II (cat.no. 10 104 159 001) contains approximately 2,000 Kunitz units per mg and comes in 100 mg size which equals 200,000 U. Resuspend in 5ml NEB recommended storage buffer, 10 mM Tris-Cl pH 7.5, 2 mM CaCl2 in 50% glycerol which equals 40,000 U/ml and store at -20°C .","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e4lqh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1db41","text":"Unit definition","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3hdqm","text":"One unit is the amount of enzyme which will completely degrade 1μg of pBR322 DNA in 10 min at 37°C in DNase I reaction buffer.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8bq6i","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"294co","text":"Reaction Buffer","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":15}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"11dl7","text":"Prepare a 10x reaction buffer containing MgCl2 and CaCl2 which are required for enzymatic activity. Final 1x buffer contains 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.6, 2.5 mM MgCl2, 0.5 mM CaCl2.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"af3lc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ca28","text":"DNase I reaction conditions","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":27}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aj2rs","text":"DNase I was tested for ability to degrade DNA in solutions containing iron chloride, EDTA-Mg ascorbate buffer and cesium chloride (see BTP Exp. 72) under conditions of 37°C for 1 hr, room temperature for 2 hr and 4°C overnight. The DNA concentration (1 Kbp DNA ladder) in each solution was 200 ng/μl. The DNase I (40,000 U/ml) was diluted 1:40 in 10x reaction buffer with final concentration of 1,000U/ml (=1 U/μl) in 10x buffer. The diluted DNase I was used at a 1:10 dilution in the DNA solutions: 1 μl DNase I in 9 μl DNA. The DNA was degraded in each solution at all the reaction conditions tested.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6o1n0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d9ktt","text":"DNase I Storage Buffer: 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.5, 2 mM CaCl2 in 50% glycerol","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":73}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"aip6v","text":"100 μl 1 M Tris-HCl, pH 7.5 (autoclaved or filter-sterilized stock)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5ajl9","text":" 20 μl 1 M CaCl2 (filter-sterilized stock)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"59gll","text":" 10 ml 50% Glycerol (autoclaved)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"190h4","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2h7n1","text":"10X DNase I Reaction Buffer: 100 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.6, 25 mM MgCl2, 5 mM
